channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New video shows Kentucky sheriff pointing gun at judge before alleged fatal shooting
item.description: Newly released surveillance footage shows when a Kentucky sheriff pointed his gun at a judge's head before allegedly shooting and killing him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rescue teams search for missing after floods, landslides kill at least 16 in Bosnia
item.description: JABLANICA, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — Rescue teams on Saturday searched for those still missing after flash floods and landslides hit parts of Bosnia, killing at least 16 people and injuring scores more.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lawyer for Diddy child accuser vows to out famous accomplices 'we all know'
item.description: The Texas lawyer who says he's representing more than dozens of potential victims of alleged sex trafficking music mogul Sean "Diddy" Combs vowed to name names in an ominous warning this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio woman's viral videos about rug buried in her yard led to search for potential body
item.description: An Ohio woman shared videos on her TikTok, concerned that a body may have been buried in her yard, leading to a police search of her property.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Well-known Asheville music tradition returns in a sign of hopefulness after Helene
item.description: A well-known Asheville musical tradition has returned in a sign of hopefulness a week after Helene battered the mountain city.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FEMA has faced criticism and praise during Helene. Here's what it does — and doesn't do
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Federal Emergency Management Agency is tasked with helping states and communities hit by disasters like Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI, DHS warn of possible Oct. 7 anniversary violence
item.description: The statement said synagogues and mosques “present attractive targets for violent attacks or for hoax threats by a variety of threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Utah State doctoral student looks to help her hometown struck by Hurricane Helene
item.description: One Utah State University student is trying to look out for her and her neighbors in North Carolina. Debris from homes and businesses after Hurricane Helene is still being cleaned up almost a week after landfall.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: Former owner of water buffalo that roamed Iowa suburb for days pleads guilty
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The owner of a water buffalo that ran loose in a Des Moines, Iowa, suburb for days pleaded guilty for having an animal at large. The owner was fined $105 and court costs Thursday, the Des Moines Register reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dozens of illegal immigrants from 'special interest' nations with terrorist activity caught at southern border
item.description: Two large groups of illegal immigrants were caught this week at the Texas-Mexico border and had dozens from "special interest" countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles mayor picks ex-sheriff to be city's police chief
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former LA County Sheriff Jim McDonnell has been selected to lead the Los Angeles Police Department, taking charge of the force of nearly 9,000 officers as discontent grows among the city's residents over public safety even as violent crime numbers drop, the mayor announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man charged with helping Idaho inmate escape during a hospital ambush sentenced to life in prison
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho man who prosecutors said ambushed and shot correctional officers at a Boise hospital in a plot to help a fellow white supremacist gang member escape from prison was sentenced to life behind bars on Friday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some perplexed at jury's mixed verdict in trial for 3 former officers in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Moments after a federal judge read the partial convictions in the federal trial of three former Memphis officers in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, an activist outside the courtroom could not contain his shock. “What the (expletive)?” Hunter Demster exclaimed to a reporter.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, KRON4

item.title: NYC Deputy Mayor Sheena Wright expected to resign: Sources
item.description: Wright joins a growing list of members of Mayor Eric Adams’ inner circle who have resigned. Banks will step down from his position on Oct. 16.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Giant bear claws out from under Southern California home
item.description: Bear break-ins in the San Gabriel foothill communities are common and most homeowners have learned to cope with the big, furry nuisances.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: TSA detects shotgun shell containing meth hidden in traveler's groin area at Pennsylvania airport
item.description: A traveler attempting to hide a prohibited substance inside a prohibited item — and then tucking them into his groin area — was quickly caught by TSA officers this week.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, Fox 5

item.title: Nigerian track star Oba Femi shines as NXT superstar
item.description: Femi will defend his title Tuesday night on the CW Network. It's a show that also features WWE legend Randy Orton.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, Fox 5

item.title: Musk to attend Trump rally at site of attempted assassination
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he plans to attend former President Donald Trump’s rally in battleground Pennsylvania this weekend when Trump returns to the site where he survived the first assassination attempt on his life.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5,000 gallons of hot asphalt spilled on Texas interstate
item.description: "We ask for you to keep the victim and their family in your thoughts and prayers," the Lindale Fire Department said.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Escatel, NewsNation

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a makeup artist.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For Pittsburgh Jews, attack anniversary adds to an already grim October
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Jewish communities everywhere reacted with horror at last year's Oct. 7 attack by Hamas on Israel, but the approaching one-year commemoration of the assault hits home particularly hard in Pittsburgh's Jewish community, which already marks a grim anniversary each October.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayorkas warns FEMA doesn't have enough funding to last through hurricane season
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says the agency tasked with helping communities recover from Hurricane Helene and other natural disasters does not have enough money to make it through the hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and COLLEEN LONG, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden student loan cancellation plan on hold again
item.description: The Biden administration’s student loan cancellation plan has been put on hold once again in Missouri after brief celebration from advocates this week when another judge ruled the plan could move forward.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: First EV 'gas station' opens in Southern California
item.description: Gas stations are great… but what if you have an electric car? A new type of service station is opening up in Santa Ana, complete with lots of EV chargers, a car wash, a WiFi lounge and even a mini-Gelsons for food and snacks.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Milwaukee man accused of stealing from same Walgreens 3 separate times
item.description: John Dzwonkowski faces charges of stealing toilet paper, soaps, lotion, razors and household cleaning items.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prosecutors say they may bring additional charges against New York City mayor and indict others
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors said Wednesday they are pursuing “several related investigations” that could lead to additional corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams and indictments for people in his orbit.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4
